criterion_selection.question: To accurately diagnose first degree AV block, which of the following diagnostic criterion should be evaluated?
options: Presence of premature atrial complexes with compensatory pauses
Presence of grouped QRS complexes separated by pauses
Complete dissociation between P waves and QRS complexes
Presence of ST-segment elevation in anterior leads

finding.question: Regarding the criterion you selected, looking at the overall rhythm, is every P wave consistently followed by a QRS complex on this ECG?

grounding.question: In which of the following segments can you observe a non-conducted P wave? The options below refer to time ranges on the ECG signal, provided in seconds.

decision.question: Based on the finding identified above, does this patient have first degree AV block?
options: Further findings are required to confirm the diagnosis
